title: Roasted Creamy Russian Fish Gratin
description: A creamy Russian dish featuring fish and potatoes, perfect for a cozy dinner on a cold evening. The fish sauce and rendered fat add a rich depth of flavor to this comforting gratin.
instructions: In a large skillet, sauté the onion and garlic in butter until softened, about 5 minutes.
Arrange a layer of potatoes in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish, overlapping them slightly.
Drizzle the rendered pork fat over the potatoes, then sprinkle with salt, black pepper, and nutmeg.
Repeat the layering process with the remaining potatoes, finishing with a layer of potatoes on top.
Spoon the fish sauce over the top layer of potatoes, then pour the heavy cream over the sauce.
Arrange the cod fillets on top of the cream, then sprinkle with dill and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 20 minutes, then remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the cod is cooked through and the potatoes are tender.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving, and serve hot on a hot stone plate.
Garnish with additional chopped dill and a dollop of sour cream, if desired.
